title: Early-Life Famine Exposure and Risk of Cardiovascular Diseases in Later Life: Findings From the REACTION Study.
ehr_evidence: The study used data from the REACTION Study, which recruited community-dwelling adults.
summary: A longitudinal study investigating the association between early-life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959-1962) and the risk of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in adults.
key_findings: Early-life famine exposure (fetal, childhood, and adolescent) significantly increased the risk of total CVD, myocardial infarction, stroke, and coronary heart disease.
Individuals in severely affected famine areas had a significantly increased risk of total CVD across all three exposed groups.
population: 259,657 community-dwelling adults aged 40 years or older from mainland China
